content.Text:
[Thu 2026-04-23 11:21 PDT] Create an "SLO Dashboard" page for this agentlens observability platform. This is a new interactive HTML page: dashboard/slo.html

**What it does:**
An interactive Service Level Objectives (SLO) dashboard where users can:
1. Define SLOs with targets (e.g., "99.9% success rate", "p95 latency < 500ms", "error rate < 1%")
2. Track error budget consumption with burn rate visualization
3. See proactive alerts when error budgets are burning too fast (multi-window burn rate analysis)
4. Get autonomous recommendations for when to freeze deployments vs when budget is healthy
5. View SLO compliance history over configurable time windows (1h, 6h, 24h, 7d, 30d)

**Key features:**
- SLO builder form: name, metric type (availability, latency, error rate, throughput), target (e.g. 99.9%), window (rolling 7d, 30d)
- Error Budget gauge (circular/donut chart showing remaining budget %)
- Burn rate chart (Canvas line chart showing budget consumption over time with 1h and 6h windows)
- Multi-window burn rate alerts: if 1h burn rate > 14.4x AND 6h burn rate > 6x → critical alert
- Autonomous recommendations panel: "Safe to deploy", "Caution - budget tightening", "FREEZE - budget exhausted"
- SLO history table with compliance status per period
- Demo data generator button that creates realistic SLO scenarios
- Export to JSON

**Technical requirements:**
- Single self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and JS (no external deps except Chart.js from CDN)
- Follow the existing dashboard style - look at dashboard/index.html and dashboard/styles.css for the design language (dark theme, CSS variables, card-based layout)
- Link it from the nav in dashboard/index.html (add "SLO" nav item)
- Also add a nav link back to index from slo.html
- Use Canvas API or Chart.js for charts
- All interactive, no server needed

**Also update dashboard/app.js:**
- Add an `slo` section to the demo data generation if there's a pattern for it, OR just keep the demo data self-contained in slo.html

**Verify:** Open slo.html and check it renders valid HTML (run: `node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const html=fs.readFileSync('dashboard/slo.html','utf8'); console.log('Lines:', html.split('\\n').length); console.log('Has canvas/chart:', html.includes('chart') || html.includes('canvas')); console.log('OK')"`)

Do NOT create a PR. Just commit changes with message: "feat(dashboard): add SLO Dashboard with error budget tracking, burn rate alerts, and autonomous deploy recommendations"
I’m checking the existing dashboard structure first so the new page matches the current visual language and nav patterns before I edit anything.
`rg` isn’t available here, so I’m falling back to direct file reads of the dashboard entry points and styles before I patch them.
